title: Comparisons between Evangelion and Terminator TSCC
display_title: Comparisons between Evangelion and Terminator TSCC
wikitext:
[]: Comparisons between Evangelion and Terminator: TSCC             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue May 26, 2009 1:04 am One of the few shows that I can honestly say has engrossed me as much as Evangelion, and possibly even more so in some ways, has been the recently canceled Terminator: The Sarah Connor Chronicles. Strangely enough, when I look back on both shows, I've started picking up on a lot of similarities between the two shows, and I can't help but wonder if it has something to do with why I like them both so much... Let's start with a comparison of two of the shows' protagonists, Shinji Ikari and John Connor. Both of them have a strong sense of destiny / fate surrounding their characters, and neither of them seems particularly enthusiastic about it. Something that is quite funny though, is that both Eva and TSCC's fandoms have accused these characters of being "emo" and "douchey" during the series' run. In TSCC there was a period where John had gone through some trauma at the start of season 2, and he was starting to detach himself from his mother, Sarah, and his cyborg guardian, Cameron, because fairly or unfairly, he blamed them for what happened to him. He spent a large chunk of season 2 running away from his destiny, and the fans started pointing the "emo asshat finger at him. Still, like Shinji he was not without redeeming or endearing qualities, even if they were occasionally being overshadowed by his anti-social emo-ness. Also, like Evangelion, TSCC digs into a lot of psychological, spiritual, and Biblical themes. The psychology of the show is all over the map, and like Evangelion it taps into some Oedipal themes, but from a different angle. In Evangelion, everyone seems to have a mommy or daddy complex (or even both), and John Connor would fit in pretty nicely with that crowd. However, his problem is not that he didn't get enough attention from his mother as a child, but rather the opposite. Sarah Conor practically smothers him with attention and is hyper protective of him, to the point that he practically goes crazy. Sarah also is a prime example of the complex, but in reverse. She is constantly threatened by the possibility that John is developing feelings for Cameron (the cyborg), and that she'll eventually be replaced by her (especially since Sarah has had a cancer scare which is haunting her in the show). There was even a set of 3 episodes that actually delved into Sarah's mind Evangelion-style to explore these issues amongst others, and ironically, much like with Evangelion, they were met with little initial enthusiasm from fans, and many people claim they were the downfall of the series. Even so, both shows dared to build complex psychological profiles around their characters, even if it wasn't "popular" to do so at the time. And lastly, I'd like to point out some interesting similarities I've noticed about my favorite character on the show, Cameron, and how she seems to relate to Evangelion. In a lot of ways, Cameron almost seems like a hybrid of Rei and Asuka. Like Rei, she does not show very much emotion, and many fans have debated whether it's because she doesn't have any, or because she keeps them bottled up and doesn't understand them. What makes her remind me of Asuka though, is that she's possessive of John similar to how Asuka is possessive of Shinji, and doesn't seem to know what she wants from him. One minute she'll be trying to seduce him, and the next, she'll be pushing him away. She's both enigmatic and manipulative, and yet at the same time, protecting John is her priority (even if it's not her end objective, which will unfortunately never be revealed), and she's even made some attempts at comforting him in the past. She's definitely a character unique to TSCC, but like I said, I can definitely see some shades of Rei and Asuka in her. Well, that's all I've got for now. If you haven't seen TSCC, I suggest you check it out sometime, even though it ended on a cliffhanger that will probably never get an official resolution. If you enjoyed Evangelion's brand of cerebral drama and deep characters, then there's a lot to like in TSCC. Unfortunately though, the more intelligent and deep the show became, the poorer its ratings got. But hey, that's what you get when you put your work in the hands of Fox, and at the mercy of an audience addicted to reality TV and sitcoms.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue May 26, 2009 11:51 am I can understand what you're getting at and I'm sad that they are not making this since I love Terminator. There are a lot of things that you can make sound like Eva if you try hard enough but ya I understand you're point.

[]:             Quote Post by GAP » Wed May 27, 2009 1:16 pm Otakon 08 Ikari wrote: I can understand what you're getting at and I'm sad that they are not making this since I love Terminator. There are a lot of things that you can make sound like Eva if you try hard enough but ya I understand you're point.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Thu May 28, 2009 2:34 am Whole thing feels like reaching for straws but have fun.
